name: Sovereign Flame Dragon
senses: blindsight 60 ft., darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 28

traits.name: Sovereign Presence
traits.desc: Any creature with less than 95% Blood Purity that starts its turn within 120 feet of the dragon must make a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened for 1 minute. Creatures with 95%+ purity are immune.

traits.name: Purity Sight
traits.desc: The dragon automatically knows the exact Blood Purity percentage of any creature it can see.

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.desc: If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: The dragon has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The dragon can use its Frightful Presence. It then makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (2d10+10) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d6+10) slashing damage.

actions.name: Tail
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (2d8+10) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Sovereign Flame Breath (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The dragon exhales fire in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw, taking 91 (26d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. This damage ignores fire resistance and treats immunity as resistance.

actions.name: Frightful Presence
actions.desc: Each creature of the dragon's choice within 120 feet must make a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.

legendary_actions.name: Detect
legendary_actions.desc: The dragon makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

legendary_actions.name: Tail Attack
legendary_actions.desc: The dragon makes a tail attack.

legendary_actions.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 15 feet must succeed on a DC 25 Dexterity saving throw or take 17 (2d6+10) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed.

lair_actions.name: Magma Surge
lair_actions.desc: A 20-foot radius pool of magma erupts at a point the dragon can see within 120 feet. Creatures in the area take 10 (3d6) fire damage per turn.

lair_actions.name: Volcanic Gases
lair_actions.desc: Poisonous gas fills a 30-foot radius sphere. Heavily obscured. Creatures entering or starting their turn there must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned.

lore: The Sovereign Flame Dragon is one of the five original Sovereigns who sacrificed their physical manifestations to anchor reality. Fragments of their essence persist in the deepest volcanic chambers of Ourobora. They recognize those of 95%+ purity as peers and will not attack unless provoked. Their flame breath bypasses all mortal resistance — only a fellow Sovereign can withstand it fully.

name: Scion Soul-Meld
damage_resistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks

traits.name: Co-Core Heartbeat
traits.desc: The Scion possesses a double heartbeat — a physical echo of the forbidden soul-meld ritual that created it. It has advantage on Constitution saving throws and death saving throws.

traits.name: Purity Hunter
traits.desc: The Scion can sense creatures with 95%+ Blood Purity within 1 mile. It covets their essence and will prioritize attacking them above all other targets. Against such targets, its attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19-20.

traits.name: Cataclysmic Strikes
traits.desc: The Scion's weapon attacks are magical. When it hits with a melee attack, it deals an additional 9 (2d8) damage of a random type (roll d4: 1=fire, 2=lightning, 3=cold, 4=necrotic).

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The Scion makes two Rend attacks and one Bite attack.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10+6) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) elemental damage.

actions.name: Rend
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6+6) slashing damage.

actions.name: Soul Shriek (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The Scion emits a psychic wail in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw, taking 45 (10d8) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Creatures with 95%+ Blood Purity have disadvantage on this save (the Scion's cry resonates with their essence).

lore: Scions are the tragic products of the forbidden soul-meld ritual — an attempt to fuse two draconic souls into a single vessel. Born with 90-94% purity, they are cataclysmic in power but forever incomplete, driven by an insatiable hunger for the pure essence of a true Sovereign. Their double heartbeat is both their gift and their curse: it sustains them beyond mortal limits, but also marks them as hunted abominations to the Church of the Five Skulls.

name: Ignis Archon Dragon
senses: blindsight 30 ft., darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 22

traits.name: Blind to Sovereigns
traits.desc: The Archon cannot detect Blood Purity above 90% — it perceives such beings as 0% Blanks. It is completely unable to recognize a Sovereign or Scion.

traits.name: Elemental Affinity (Fire)
traits.desc: When the Archon casts a spell that deals fire damage, it adds its Charisma modifier (+4) to the damage.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The Archon makes two attacks: one Bite and one Claw.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d10+7) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d6+7) slashing damage.

actions.name: Ignis Breath (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The dragon exhales fire in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 63 (18d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

lore: Ignis Archons are the ruling elite's enforcers — fire-aligned dragons in service to House Ignis. They maintain order through scorched-earth tactics, burning entire villages suspected of harboring Cult of the Sixth sympathizers. Despite their power, they are tragically limited: their biology prevents them from sensing true Sovereign purity. An awakened Sovereign could walk among them unnoticed, a fact the Church suppresses at all costs.

name: Aegis-Inquisitor

traits.name: Draconic Retribution
traits.desc: When the Inquisitor hits with a melee weapon attack, it adds 1d4 fire or lightning damage (its choice). This is a manifestation of its 40-60% Blood Purity.

traits.name: Purity Sight
traits.desc: As a bonus action, the Inquisitor can read the exact Blood Purity percentage of a creature it can see within 30 feet. This ability requires concentration.

traits.name: Spell Safety (40% Purity)
traits.desc: The Inquisitor's Spell Safety DC is 12. When casting a spell of 1st level or higher, it rolls a Constitution saving throw against DC 12. On failure, it takes 1d6 fire damage per spell level (ignores resistance). It has advantage on this save.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The Inquisitor makes two Longsword attacks.

actions.name: Longsword
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8+3) slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) fire damage.

actions.name: Inquisitorial Smite (1/Day)
actions.desc: The Inquisitor channels divine fury into a melee attack. On a hit, the target takes an additional 27 (6d8) radiant damage and must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be stunned until the end of the Inquisitor's next turn. This ability is reserved for capital crimes: illegal spellcasting or harboring a false purity reading.

lore: Aegis-Inquisitors are the Church of the Five Skulls' enforcers — Paladin-War Cleric hybrids ordained to hunt illegal casters and suppress heresy. Each Inquisitor carries a portable Blood Crystal for field purity testing. They are trained from childhood in House Obsidian's judicial academies, where they learn that mercy is weakness and purging the impure is the highest form of worship.

name: Penitent Zealot

traits.name: The Fervor Risk
traits.desc: As a bonus action, the Zealot can enter a state of reckless fervor. For 1 minute, it gains advantage on all melee attack rolls, but takes 1d4 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. This damage cannot be reduced or prevented. The Zealot can end this state as a bonus action.

traits.name: Cult Conditioning
traits.desc: The Zealot has advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened by Church authorities or Inquisitors. It has been conditioned to resist their influence through years of underground resistance.

actions.name: Sump-Forged Blade
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6+3) slashing damage. When the Zealot's Fervor Risk is active, this attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 19-20.

actions.name: Throwing Dagger
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) piercing damage.

actions.name: Desperate Smite (Recharges after Fervor Risk ends)
actions.desc: While Fervor Risk is active, the Zealot can channel its pain into a single devastating strike. Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes an additional 14 (4d6) radiant damage, and the Zealot's Fervor Risk ends immediately. The Zealot takes 7 (2d6) necrotic damage from the feedback.

lore: Penitent Zealots are the shock troops of the Cult of the Sixth — radicalized laborers, escaped Blanks, and those who've lost everything to the purity caste system. They fight with a desperate courage born of having nothing left to lose. The Fervor Risk is their signature technique: a controlled Aether Burn channeled into martial fury. They hum fragments of the 'Dig Deep' work chant as they fight, each verse a prayer for the Sixth Sovereign's return.

name: Abomination — Stitched

traits.name: Sovereign-Aggro
traits.desc: If the Stitched can see a creature with 95%+ Blood Purity, it MUST attack that creature on its turn if able. It ignores all other targets. It has advantage on attack rolls against creatures with 95%+ purity.

traits.name: Unstable Flesh
traits.desc: When the Stitched takes slashing damage, creatures within 5 feet must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 3 (1d6) acid damage from sprayed alchemical fluids.

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: The Stitched regains 5 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point. This regeneration is suppressed for 1 round if the Stitched takes fire or radiant damage.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The Stitched makes two Slam attacks. It may replace one with Graft-Tear.

actions.name: Slam
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8+5) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Graft-Tear
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6+5) slashing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or have its maximum hit points reduced by the damage taken until it finishes a long rest. The Stitched absorbs the stolen vitality, gaining temporary hit points equal to the damage dealt.

lore: Fresh Stitched Abominations are the results of recent illegal alchemical experiments — typically by House Tenebris flesh-shapers or rogue Cult scientists. They are mindless, driven solely by hunger and an instinctive homing sense toward uncorrupted Sovereign essence. A Stitched will bulldoze through walls, allies, and obstacles to reach a 95%+ purity being, making them both terrifying weapons and uncontrollable liabilities. Their bodies leak alchemical slurry that burns on contact.

name: Abomination — Titan
damage_resistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks

traits.name: Sovereign-Aggro (Enhanced)
traits.desc: The Titan can sense creatures with 95%+ Blood Purity within 500 feet. It MUST move toward and attack such a creature if able. It has advantage on all attack rolls against 95%+ purity targets and deals an additional 3d6 damage to them.

traits.name: Titanic Regeneration
traits.desc: The Titan regains 15 hit points at the start of its turn. Only radiant damage suppresses this regeneration until the end of the Titan's next turn.

traits.name: Aether Absorption
traits.desc: When the Titan is targeted by a spell, the caster must make a DC 18 Spell Safety check (Constitution saving throw). On a failure, the spell is absorbed — it has no effect, and the Titan regains hit points equal to twice the spell's level. This triggers regardless of the caster's Purity.

traits.name: Unstoppable
traits.desc: The Titan cannot be grappled, restrained, or shoved. It ignores difficult terrain.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The Titan makes three attacks: one Bite and two Crushing Limbs.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d8+8) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) acid damage.

actions.name: Crushing Limb
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d6+8) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a DC 20 Strength saving throw or be grappled.

actions.name: Acid Torrent (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The Titan vomits alchemical waste in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 (10d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The area becomes difficult terrain coated in corrosive sludge — creatures starting their turn there take 7 (2d6) acid damage.

legendary_actions.name: Charge
legendary_actions.desc: The Titan moves up to half its speed toward the nearest creature with 95%+ Blood Purity without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary_actions.name: Regurgitate (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The Titan ejects a smaller Abomination (Stitched, 1 HP remaining) into an unoccupied space within 20 feet. This creature acts on initiative count 0.

lore: Titans are the 'successful' Abominations — stable enough to function as war machines but insane enough to be uncontrollable. Created by House Tenebris during the Flesh Wars, each Titan was stitched from dozens of failed Archon candidates fused with animal tissue and alchemical binding agents. Only one in twenty survived the process with mobility; only one in fifty achieved the horrifying intelligence of a Titan. They were abandoned in the northern wastes when they proved more dangerous to their creators than their enemies. Titans retain one directive burned into what passes for their consciousness: CONSUME THE PURE.

name: Obsidian Blood-Judge

traits.name: Purity Sight (Greater)
traits.desc: As a bonus action, the Blood-Judge reads the exact Blood Purity percentage of any creature within 60 feet. It can also detect if a purity reading has been magically altered or forged.

traits.name: Law-Bender
traits.desc: The Blood-Judge has a permanent +2 bonus to all Deception and Insight checks. It can cast Zone of Truth (DC 16) at will without components or Spell Safety risk (this is a Church-granted ability, not spellcasting).

traits.name: Judicial Authority
traits.desc: If the Blood-Judge declares a creature guilty of heresy or illegal spellcasting, all allied Inquisitors within 60 feet gain advantage on attack rolls against that creature for 1 minute.

actions.name: Glass-Blade Rapier
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8+2) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage. The psychic damage represents the weight of judgment.

actions.name: Sentence (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The Blood-Judge pronounces a sentence on a creature it can see within 60 feet. The target must make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On failure, the Blood-Judge selects one: *Guilty* — target is paralyzed for 1 minute (save ends at end of each turn); *Impure* — target takes 28 (8d6) radiant damage and its Spell Safety DC increases by 5 for 1 hour; *Heretic* — target is marked. All Church-affiliated creatures have advantage on tracking and attacking it for 24 hours.

lore: Blood-Judges are the legal arm of House Obsidian and the Church. They preside over Ascension ceremonies, purity hearings, and heresy trials. A Blood-Judge's word is execution — they can sentence anyone below Archon caste without trial. Above Archon, they must convene a tribunal of three Judges. Their glass-blade rapiers are forged from the same Blood Crystal material used in Ascension testing; each strike resonates with the victim's own purity, causing psychic backlash proportionate to how 'impure' the Judge has declared them.

name: Sump Drake

traits.name: Sovereign Cower
traits.desc: If a creature with 95%+ Blood Purity is within 30 feet, the Drake must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On failure, it is frightened and must use its movement to retreat. It repeats the save at the end of each turn.

traits.name: Amphibious
traits.desc: The Drake can breathe air and water.

traits.name: Swamp Camouflage
traits.desc: The Drake has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in swampy terrain or murky water.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8+4) piercing damage.

actions.name: Tail Sweep
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) bludgeoning damage. Target must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

actions.name: Acid Spit (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 30/60 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (4d8) acid damage. The target's AC is reduced by 1 until it takes an action to wipe off the corrosive sludge.

lore: Sump Drakes are the most common lesser dragon in the inhabited regions of Solis-Grave. They infest the canal systems, sump-pits, and underground waterways beneath the great cities. Adult Sump Drakes reach 12 feet in length and hunt in mated pairs, dragging prey into murky water to drown before feeding. Their instinctive terror of Sovereign-class beings is absolute — a quirk of their mutated draconic ancestry that even the Church cannot explain. Sump-Blood canal-runners have learned to carry Sovereign-fragments (counterfeit or real) as drake repellent.

name: Crystal-Bound Lich
damage_immunities: poison; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks

traits.name: Crystal Phylactery
traits.desc: The Lich's soul is bound to a Pristine Blood Crystal hidden somewhere in the world. If the Lich is destroyed, it reforms near its phylactery in 1d10 days unless the phylactery is shattered (requires a DC 20 Strength check or 50+ damage in a single strike).

traits.name: Turn Resistance
traits.desc: The Lich has advantage on saving throws against any effect that turns undead.

traits.name: Spell Safety Mastery (85% Purity)
traits.desc: The Lich's Spell Safety DC is 3. It has advantage on Spell Safety checks. On the rare failure, Aether Burn damages its crystal phylactery instead of its body — adding a unique risk/reward to its spellcasting.

actions.name: Paralyzing Touch
actions.desc: Melee Spell Attack: +13 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 10 (3d6) cold damage. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be paralyzed for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.

actions.name: Crystal Shard (Cantrip)
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +13 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (4d6) piercing damage from crystallized Aether. No Spell Safety check required (cantrip).

actions.name: Aether Storm (Recharge 5-6, 5th-level)
actions.desc: The Lich casts a storm of raw Aether in a 30-foot radius. Each creature in the area must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 (10d8) damage of a random type (d4: 1=fire, 2=lightning, 3=cold, 4=necrotic) on a failed save. The Lich must succeed on a DC 3 Spell Safety check (CON save, advantage) or the damage rebounds: half the damage dealt is also dealt to the Lich's phylactery (tracked separately by DM).

legendary_actions.name: Cantrip
legendary_actions.desc: The Lich casts Crystal Shard.

legendary_actions.name: Phylactery Pulse (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The Lich's phylactery pulses, healing the Lich for 15 hit points. If at full HP, it gains 15 temporary hit points instead.

legendary_actions.name: Disrupt Purity (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The Lich targets one creature it can see within 60 feet. The target must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On failure, its effective Blood Purity is reduced by 20 (to a minimum of 0%) for 1 hour. If the target is casting a spell, the Spell Safety DC increases by 5 for this casting.

lair_actions.name: Crystal Resonance
lair_actions.desc: All Blood Crystals in the lair hum with unstable energy. Each creature with 15%+ Blood Purity takes 5 (1d10) psychic damage.

lair_actions.name: Summon Shards
lair_actions.desc: Animated Blood Crystal Shards (CR 4, see separate entry) rise from the floor in 1d4 unoccupied spaces.

lore: Crystal-Bound Liches are the ultimate expression of Archon hubris — ancient casters who bound their souls to Pristine Blood Crystals to cheat death. Unlike traditional liches who feed on souls, these undead sustain themselves through their crystal's connection to ambient Aether. They are not inherently evil, but centuries of isolation and the slow corruption of the crystal-phylactery drives most to madness. Their Aether Storm spell is legendary: a controlled Aether Burn directed outward. The risk that the damage rebounds to the phylactery is the Lich's eternal gamble — one day, the crystal will shatter.

name: goblin scout

traits.name: Nimble Escape
traits.desc: Can Disengage or Hide as a bonus action.

actions.name: Scimitar
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6+2) slashing damage.

actions.name: Shortbow
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6+2) piercing damage.

lore: Goblin scouts prowl the borderlands, stealing from caravans.

name: orc warrior

traits.name: Aggressive
traits.desc: Can move up to its speed towards a hostile creature as a bonus action.

actions.name: Greataxe
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d12+3) slashing damage.

lore: Orc warriors are brutal raiders in the ash wastes.

name: town guard

traits.name: Sentinel
traits.desc: Opportunity attacks hit and reduce speed to 0.

actions.name: Spear
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6+2) piercing damage.

actions.name: Crossbow
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8+1) piercing damage.

lore: Desperate guards protect crumbling towns in Solis-Grave.

name: cult fanatic

traits.name: Dark Devotion
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws vs. charm and frighten.

actions.name: Dagger
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) piercing damage.

actions.name: Bane
actions.desc: Spell (DC 12), target must save or subtract 1d4 from attacks for 1 min.

lore: Mad devotees of forgotten sun-gods spread decay.

name: aether-warped wolf

traits.name: Pack Tactics
traits.desc: Advantage on attacks if ally is within 5 ft.

traits.name: Aetheric Instability
traits.desc: When hit with magical damage, emits 5 ft. energy burst (DC 12, 1d4 force).

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8+2) piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) force damage.

lore: Twisted by leaking aether, these wolves glow faintly.

name: giant spider
senses: blindsight 10 ft., darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 10

traits.name: Spider Climb
traits.desc: Can climb difficult surfaces without ability checks.

traits.name: Web Walker
traits.desc: Ignores web movement restrictions.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) piercing damage plus poison (DC 12, 2d6 poison).

actions.name: Web
actions.desc: Ranged Attack +5, restrained (DC 12 escape), 50 ft. range.

lore: Web-choked ruins are home to these silent hunters.

name: bandit captain

traits.name: Parry
traits.desc: Adds 2 to AC against melee attack.

actions.name: Scimitar
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6+4) slashing damage.

actions.name: Leadership
actions.desc: Allies within 30 ft. get +1d4 on attacks and saves for 1 min.

lore: Leaders of desperate brigands in ash-covered roads.

name: young sump drake
senses: blindsight 10 ft., darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 14

traits.name: Amphibious
traits.desc: Can breathe air and water.

traits.name: Stench
traits.desc: Creatures within 10 ft. have disadvantage on attack rolls.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6+3) piercing plus 2 (1d4) acid.

actions.name: Acid Breath
actions.desc: Recharge 5-6, 15 ft. cone, DC 13 Dex, 4d6 acid damage, half on save.

lore: Sump drakes lurk in polluted marshes, spitting acid.

name: inquisitor acolyte

traits.name: Inquisitive Judgment
traits.desc: Advantage on saves vs. illusion.

actions.name: Mace
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6+2) bludgeoning plus 4 (1d8) radiant.

actions.name: Burning Light
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, 60 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d8+3) radiant.

lore: Zealots of the Solis-Grave Inquisition root out heresy.

name: blank laborer-turned-brigand

traits.name: Brute Force
traits.desc: Melee weapon attacks deal extra 2 (1d4) bludgeoning.

actions.name: Sledgehammer
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6+3) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Improvised Throw
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) bludgeoning.

lore: Broken workers turned to banditry in Solis-Grave.

name: Giant Rat (Aether-touched)
traits: Keen Smell: Advantage on perception checks involving smell.
Aether Resonance: Teleport 10 ft. as a bonus action (recharge 5-6).
actions: Bite: +4 to hit, 1 piercing + 1 force damage.

name: Goblin Boss
traits: Nimble Escape: Disengage or Hide as a bonus action.
Gang Up: Allies within 5 ft. have advantage on attack rolls.
actions: Scimitar: +4 to hit, 1d6+2 slashing.
Shoot Arrow: +4 to hit, 1d6+2 piercing.

name: Orc Eye of the Sixth
traits: Aggressive: Move toward enemy as bonus action.
Sixth Sense: Advantage on initiative rolls.
actions: Greatclub: +5 to hit, 1d8+3 bludgeoning.

name: Cult Recruiter
traits: Silver Tongue: Persuasion and deception checks at advantage vs creatures who haven't attacked it.
actions: Dagger: +3 to hit, 1d4+1 piercing.

name: Ash-Walker Outcast
traits: Ash Cloud: When hit, a cloud of ash blinds nearby creatures (DC 13 CON save) for 1 round.
actions: Slam: +5 to hit, 2d6+3 bludgeoning + 1d6 fire.
Cinder Burst (Recharge 5-6): 15 ft. cone, DC 13 DEX, 3d6 fire damage.

name: Stone-Blood Smith
traits: Stone Endurance: Reduce damage taken by 1d12+2 once per short rest.
actions: Warhammer: +5 to hit, 1d10+3 bludgeoning.

name: Bone-Wrought Drone
damage_resistances: piercing and slashing from nonmagical weapons
traits: Death Burst: Upon destruction, releases bone shards in a 5 ft. radius, DC 11 DEX, 2d4 piercing.
actions: Claw: +3 to hit, 1d6+1 slashing.

name: Sump-Canal Crocodile
traits: Water Camouflage: Advantage on stealth in water.
actions: Bite: +5 to hit, 1d10+3 piercing, target grappled (escape DC 14).
Death Roll: Grappled target takes 2d8+3 bludgeoning damage, and crocodile can't bite another until grapple ends.

name: Ferrum Recruit
traits: Iron Discipline: Allies within 10 ft. gain +1 to saving throws against fear.
actions: Longsword: +4 to hit, 1d8+2 slashing.
Javelin: +4 to hit (range 30/120), 1d6+2 piercing.

name: Pyre Apprentice

Cantrips (at will): guidance, light, mending, thaumaturgy
1st level (4 slots): command, cure wounds, guiding bolt, shield of faith
2nd level (3 slots): hold person, spiritual weapon, zone of truth
3rd level (3 slots): dispel magic, spirit guardians, speak with dead
4th level (3 slots): banishment, death ward, divination

2nd level (3 slots): gust of wind, misty step, shatter
3rd level (3 slots): call lightning, fly, lightning bolt

actions.description: The abyss-walker makes three attacks: one with its tendril and two with its claws.

actions.name: Tendril
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8+5) slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) necrotic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 17). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the abyss-walker can't use its tendril on another target.

actions.name: Claw
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6+6) slashing damage.

actions.name: Void Collapse (Recharge 5-6)
actions.description: The abyss-walker creates a 20-foot-radius sphere of absolute darkness centered on a point it can see within 60 feet. The sphere spreads around corners. It is considered heavily obscured, and any creature that starts its turn inside the sphere must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw, taking 36 (8d8) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The sphere lasts for 1 minute, until the abyss-walker dismisses it (no action required), or until it uses this action again.

description: A hunched, gangling form of shifting shadows and writhing tendrils. Its presence seems to drink the light from the air.

traits.name: Penitent's Aura
traits.desc: Any creature within 30 feet of the high-priest that can hear it must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw at the start of its turn or be paralyzed until the start of its next turn. A creature that succeeds is immune to this aura for 24 hours. Undead and constructs are immune.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc:
The high-priest is a 14th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 17, +9 to hit with spell attacks). It has the following cleric spells prepared:
Cantrips (at will): guidance, mending, thaumaturgy, toll the dead
1st level (4 slots): bane, command, inflict wounds, shield of faith
2nd level (3 slots): hold person, silence, spiritual weapon
3rd level (3 slots): bestow curse, dispel magic, spirit guardians (radiant)
4th level (3 slots): banishment, death ward, divination
5th level (2 slots): contagion, geas
6th level (1 slot): heal
7th level (1 slot): symbol (pain)

traits.name: Divine Strike
traits.desc: Once per turn when the high-priest hits a creature with a weapon attack, it can cause the attack to deal an extra 2d8 radiant damage. If the target is a fiend or undead, it takes an extra 4d8 radiant damage instead.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The high-priest makes two melee attacks.

actions.name: Mace
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 1) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) radiant damage.

actions.name: Penitent's Gaze (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The high-priest targets one creature it can see within 60 feet. The target must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 28 (8d6) psychic damage and is blinded for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half as much damage and is not blinded. A blind creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

legendary_actions.name: Cantrip
legendary_actions.desc: The high-priest casts a cantrip.

legendary_actions.name: Divine Retribution (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The high-priest forces one creature within 30 feet that dealt damage to it since its last turn to make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 14 (4d6) psychic damage and is frightened until the end of its next turn.

legendary_actions.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The high-priest casts a spell of 3rd level or lower from its spell list.

name: Elder Wyvern
senses: blindsight 60 ft., darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 19

traits.name: Acidic Blood
traits.desc: Whenever a creature hits the elder wyvern with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it, the attacker takes 7 (2d6) acid damage.

traits.name: Flyby
traits.desc: The elder wyvern doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

traits.name: Poisonous Stinger
traits.desc: The wyvern's tail stinger deals an extra 5 (1d10) poison damage on a hit, and the target must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned in this way, the target also takes 7 (2d6) poison damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The wyvern makes two attacks: one with its bite and one with its stinger. While flying, it can use its claws in place of one of these attacks.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 7) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claws
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 7) slashing damage.

actions.name: Stinger
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 7) piercing damage plus 5 (1d10) poison damage, and the target must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned as described in the trait.

actions.name: Toxic Breath (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The wyvern exhales a cloud of poison in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw, taking 42 (12d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary_actions.name: Detect
legendary_actions.desc: The wyvern makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

legendary_actions.name: Tail Swipe (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The wyvern sweeps its tail in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or take 10 (2d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone.

legendary_actions.name: Wing Attack (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The wyvern beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or take 12 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The wyvern can then fly up to half its flying speed.

name: Crystal-Infused Hydra
senses: darkvision 60 ft., tremorsense 30 ft., passive Perception 21

traits.name: Crystalline Growth
traits.desc: At the start of each of its turns, the hydra grows one new head for each head lost since its last turn (max 10 heads). Each head has 25 hit points. While the hydra has more than one head, it has advantage on saving throws against being blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and knocked unconscious.

traits.name: Crystal Shard Regeneration
traits.desc: If the hydra takes cold damage, it regrows one head at the start of its next turn. If it takes radiant damage, it loses one head (if it has any) and its maximum hit points are reduced by 5 per head lost until it finishes a long rest.

traits.name: Multiheaded
traits.desc: The hydra can make as many bite attacks per round as it has heads. It can also use its Crystal Spray action as many times as it has heads, but each head can only use it once per turn.

traits.name: Reflective Scales
traits.desc: Any time a creature targets the hydra with a spell that requires a ranged spell attack, roll a d6. On a 1-2, the spell is reflected back at the caster as if the caster were the target.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The hydra makes as many bite attacks as it has heads, and can use Crystal Spray up to that many times.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage plus 5 (1d10) radiant damage.

actions.name: Crystal Spray
actions.desc: The hydra spews a spray of razor-sharp crystal shards from one of its heads in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) slashing damage and 10 (3d6) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary_actions.name: Head Regrowth
legendary_actions.desc: The hydra regrows one head (up to maximum). It gains 25 hit points.

legendary_actions.name: Crystal Burst (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: All heads that are able to do so release a burst of crystal shards. Each creature within 20 feet of the hydra must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) slashing damage and becoming blinded until the end of its next turn on a failed save.

legendary_actions.name: Rampage (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The hydra moves up to its speed and makes a bite attack with each head.

name: Church Purifier Golem
damage_immunities: fire, poison, psychic, bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks that aren't adamantine
condition_immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

traits.name: Holy Furnace
traits.desc: Any creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the golem takes 7 (2d6) fire damage plus 7 (2d6) radiant damage. Undead and fiends take double the radiant damage.

traits.name: Immunity to Magic
traits.desc: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form or control it. Additionally, spell attacks against it have disadvantage.

traits.name: Magic Weapons
traits.desc: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

traits.name: Purifying Aura
traits.desc: All nonmagical flames within 30 feet of the golem are extinguished. Magical fire is suppressed within the aura and cannot create new fire.

traits.name: Stalwart
traits.desc: The golem cannot be knocked prone and has advantage on saving throws against being moved against its will.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The golem makes two slam attacks and uses its Cleansing Breath if available.

actions.name: Slam
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) radiant damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 18 Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked prone.

actions.name: Cleansing Breath (Recharge 5-6)
actions.desc: The golem exhales a 30-foot cone of purifying fire. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 27 (6d8) fire damage plus 27 (6d8) radiant damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage. Undead and fiends have disadvantage on this saving throw.

legendary_actions.name: Slam
legendary_actions.desc: The golem makes one slam attack.

legendary_actions.name: Radiant Burst (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The golem emits a burst of radiant energy. Each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) radiant damage and being blinded until the end of its next turn on a failed save.

legendary_actions.name: Heat Wave (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The golem intensifies its Holy Furnace. Until the start of its next turn, the damage from its Holy Furnace trait increases to 14 (4d6) fire damage plus 14 (4d6) radiant damage.

name: Tenebris Flesh-Weaver
senses: blindsight 60 ft., darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 17

traits.name: Fleshweaving Aura
traits.desc: Any creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of the flesh-weaver must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or have one of its limbs (determined randomly) become horribly contorted, reducing its speed by 10 feet and imposing disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn. This effect is cumulative; a creature that fails multiple times loses use of that limb entirely (speed halved, can't use two-handed weapons, etc.) until it receives a greater restoration spell or similar magic.

traits.name: Amorphous
traits.desc: The flesh-weaver can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: The flesh-weaver has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: The flesh-weaver regains 15 hit points at the start of its turn. If it takes fire or radiant damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. The flesh-weaver dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The flesh-weaver makes three attacks: one with its tendril, one with its claw, and one with its bite, or two with its Flesh Reave.

actions.name: Tendril
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) necrotic damage. The target is grappled (escape DC 18) and pulled up to 10 feet closer. While grappled, the target is restrained and takes 9 (2d8) necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (3d6 + 5) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) poison damage. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned in this way, the target is also paralyzed. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

actions.name: Flesh Reave
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 60 ft., one creature. Hit: 22 (5d8) necrotic damage and the target must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or have its hit point maximum reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest or receives a greater restoration spell. The flesh-weaver regains hit points equal to half the damage dealt.

legendary_actions.name: Tendril Lash
legendary_actions.desc: The flesh-weaver makes one tendril attack.

legendary_actions.name: Weave Flesh (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The flesh-weaver targets a creature grappled by it. The target must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, one of its body parts (determined randomly) is horribly reshaped: its speed is halved, or it loses the use of a hand, or its vision is impaired (disadvantage on Perception checks using sight), etc. The effect can be removed by a greater restoration spell.

legendary_actions.name: Necrotic Burst (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary_actions.desc: The flesh-weaver releases a wave of dark energy. Each creature within 20 feet of it must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. The flesh-weaver regains hit points equal to the total damage dealt.

name: Scion Eclipse-Born
damage_resistances: necrotic, radiant; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
senses: darkvision 120 ft., truesight 60 ft., passive Perception 18

traits.name: Eclipse Aura
traits.description: At the start of each of the scion's turns, each creature within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 19 Constitution saving throw or take 7 (2d6) necrotic damage and be blinded until the start of the scion's next turn. Undead and constructs are immune to this effect.

traits.name: Shadow Step
traits.description: As a bonus action, the scion can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see, provided that space is in dim light or darkness.

traits.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
traits.description: While in sunlight, the scion has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.description: The scion's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 19). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components: At will: darkness, detect magic, shield; 3/day: blight, counterspell; 1/day: antilife shell, etherealness.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The scion makes three attacks: two with its Shadow Claw and one with its Eclipse Ray, or it can use its Ray of Annihilation instead of the Eclipse Ray.

actions.name: Shadow Claw
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8+5) slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 19 Constitution saving throw or its maximum hit points are reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest.

actions.name: Eclipse Ray
actions.description: Ranged Spell Attack: +12 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d10) radiant damage. The target must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute. A blinded creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

actions.name: Ray of Annihilation (Recharge 5-6)
actions.description: The scion fires a beam of utter darkness in a 60-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 36 (8d8) necrotic damage and is teleported to a random location on the plane of Shadow (or a similar dark plane) for 1 minute. On a success, it takes half damage and is not teleported. At the end of each of its turns, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

legendary_actions.name: Detect
legendary_actions.description: The scion makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

legendary_actions.name: Shadow Reave (2 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The scion teleports up to 30 feet and makes one Shadow Claw attack against a creature within reach.

legendary_actions.name: Eclipse Burst (3 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The scion unleashes a burst of shadowy energy. Each creature within 20 feet of it must make a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) necrotic damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Undead and constructs are immune.

name: Obsidian Law-Golem
damage_immunities: poison, psychic; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks that aren't adamantine
condition_immunities: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, stunned
senses: darkvision 120 ft., truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 19
languages: all languages known to its creator but cannot speak

traits.name: Immutable Form
traits.description: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.description: The golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

traits.name: Eldritch Enforcement
traits.description: When the golem hits a creature with a melee attack, it can force the target to make a DC 19 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target is bound by a magical contract etched into the golem's surface. The target must obey the golem's commands for 1 hour, as if under the effects of the geas spell. While bound, the target cannot attack the golem or its allies, and it takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage at the start of each of its turns if it disobeys. The effect ends early if the golem uses this trait on another creature or if the golem is destroyed.

traits.name: Edict of Stone
traits.description: The golem's weapon attacks are magical and deal an extra 4d8 force damage (included in the attack).

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The golem makes two slam attacks.

actions.name: Slam
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 25 (3d10+7) bludgeoning damage plus 18 (4d8) force damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 21 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone and restrained (escape DC 21).

actions.name: Force Beam (Recharge 5-6)
actions.description: The golem emits a beam of pure force in a 90-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that area must make a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 42 (12d6) force damage and is pushed 20 feet away from the golem. On a success, it takes half damage and is not pushed.

legendary_actions.name: Slam
legendary_actions.description: The golem makes one slam attack.

legendary_actions.name: Compel Compliance (2 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The golem targets one creature it can see within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 19 Charisma saving throw or be affected by the golem's Eldritch Enforcement trait.

legendary_actions.name: Obsidian Shield (3 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The golem creates a barrier of obsidian around itself. Until the start of its next turn, it gains a +4 bonus to AC and resistance to all damage except force.

name: Sovereign Flame Whelp
senses: blindsight 60 ft., darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 24

traits.name: Crown of Pyres
traits.description: At the start of each of the whelp's turns, each creature within 20 feet of it must succeed on a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw or take 7 (2d6) fire damage. A creature that fails is also set on fire (taking 3d6 fire damage at the start of each of its turns until it uses an action to extinguish the flames).

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.description: If the whelp fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Sovereign Aura
traits.description: The whelp radiates an aura of domination. Any creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of the whelp must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by the whelp until the start of its next turn. While charmed in this way, the creature cannot target the whelp or its allies, and it must use its action to move toward the whelp if able.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The whelp makes one bite attack and two claw attacks.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10+5) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6+5) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

actions.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6)
actions.description: The whelp exhales fire in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 56 (16d6) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. A creature that fails is also knocked prone and set on fire (as per Crown of Pyres).

actions.name: Infernal Word (1/Day)
actions.description: The whelp utters a word of power. Each creature of its choice within 60 feet that can hear it must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature is stunned for 1 minute. A stunned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

legendary_actions.name: Detect
legendary_actions.description: The whelp makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

legendary_actions.name: Tail Attack (2 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The whelp makes a tail attack. Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8+5) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. The target must succeed on a DC 19 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

legendary_actions.name: Flame Burst (3 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The whelp releases a burst of fire from its body. Each creature within 30 feet of it must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Creatures that fail are also set on fire.

name: Bone Colossus
damage_resistances: necrotic; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
senses: darkvision 120 ft., tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 19
languages: understands the languages of its creator but cannot speak

traits.name: Bone Plating
traits.description: The colossus has a thick armor of bones. Any critical hit against it becomes a normal hit. Additionally, when the colossus is hit by a melee attack, the attacker takes 5 (1d10) piercing damage from bone shards.

traits.name: Undead Fortitude
traits.description: If damage reduces the colossus to 0 hit points, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5 + the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, it drops to 1 hit point instead.

traits.name: Deathly Stench
traits.description: Any creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of the colossus must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. While poisoned in this way, the creature's speed is halved and it cannot take reactions.

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.description: The colossus regains 15 hit points at the start of its turn. If it takes radiant damage, this trait does not function on its next turn. The colossus can be permanently destroyed only by radiant damage or by being reduced to 0 hit points while under the effect of a hallow spell.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The colossus makes two slam attacks, and then it can use its Bone Storm if available.

actions.name: Slam
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 32 (4d10+8) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) necrotic damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 23 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

actions.name: Bone Storm (Recharge 5-6)
actions.description: The colossus unleashes a whirlwind of bone shards in a 60-foot radius around itself. Each creature in that area must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) piercing damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Additionally, the area becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute, and any creature that enters it for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes 7 (2d6) piercing damage.

actions.name: Grave Grasp (1/Day)
actions.description: The colossus targets one creature it can see within 60 feet. That creature must succeed on a DC 22 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 40 feet toward the colossus and then grappled (escape DC 23). While grappled, the target is restrained and takes 14 (4d6) necrotic damage at the start of each of the colossus's turns. The colossus can only grapple one creature at a time.

legendary_actions.name: Slam
legendary_actions.description: The colossus makes one slam attack.

legendary_actions.name: Bone Shard Burst (2 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The colossus causes bone shards to explode from its body. Each creature within 15 feet must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) piercing damage on a failure, or half on a success.

legendary_actions.name: Deathly Gaze (3 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The colossus fixes its empty eye sockets on one creature within 60 feet. The target must make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) necrotic damage on a failure, or half on a success. Additionally, on a failure, the target's maximum hit points are reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken, and the colossus regains that many hit points. This reduction lasts until the creature finishes a long rest.

name: Void-Touched Horror
damage_resistances: cold, lightning, psychic; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
condition_immunities: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, stunned
senses: blindsight 120 ft., truesight 60 ft., passive Perception 20
languages: Deep Speech, telepathy 120 ft., understands all languages but does not speak

traits.name: Void Aura
traits.description: At the start of each of the horror's turns, each creature within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 21 Constitution saving throw or take 10 (3d6) psychic damage and be unable to regain hit points until the start of the horror's next turn. Undead and constructs are immune.

traits.name: Alien Mind
traits.description: The horror is immune to any spell or effect that would detect its emotions or read its thoughts, and it cannot be targeted by divination magic.

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.description: If the horror fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting (Psionics)
traits.description: The horror's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 21). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: detect thoughts, dissonant whispers (4th level), phantasmal force; 3/day: confusion, synaptic static; 1/day: feeblemind, power word stun.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The horror makes three attacks: two with its Void Tendrils and one with its Mind-Warping Gaze.

actions.name: Void Tendrils
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6+5) necrotic damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 21 Intelligence saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

actions.name: Mind-Warping Gaze
actions.description: Ranged Spell Attack: +13 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (5d6+5) psychic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw or suffer a random form of short-term madness (see DMG) for 1 minute.

actions.name: Void Eruption (Recharge 5-6)
actions.description: The horror unleashes a wave of pure void energy in a 60-foot radius sphere centered on itself. Each creature in that area must make a DC 21 Intelligence saving throw, taking 45 (10d8) psychic damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. On a failure, the target is also afflicted with a random form of indefinite madness (see DMG).

legendary_actions.name: Telekinetic Shove
legendary_actions.description: The horror targets one creature it can see within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 21 Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 30 feet in a direction of the horror's choice and knocked prone.

legendary_actions.name: Void Lash (2 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The horror makes one Void Tendril attack.

legendary_actions.name: Psychic Scream (3 actions)
legendary_actions.description: The horror emits a psychic scream in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 21 Intelligence saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) psychic damage and being deafened for 1 minute on a failure, or half damage and no deafness on a success.
